title: oxalis griffithii var. kantoensis
intro: Oxalis griffithii var. kantoensis, also known as the Japanese wood sorrel, is a variety of the Oxalis griffithii plant species, belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and gastritis, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which may help to boost the immune system and reduce the risk of infections. Herbal preparations of Oxalis griffithii var. kantoensis include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
